questions.q: Explain the role of the Planning Commission in India's development process. How did it help in achieving the goals of planned development?
questions.answer: The Planning Commission played a crucial role in India's development process by formulating Five-Year Plans to achieve the goals of planned development. It helped in allocating resources, setting priorities, and coordinating the efforts of various ministries and departments. The Commission also monitored the progress of the plans and made necessary adjustments. Through the plans, the Commission aimed to promote rapid economic growth, reduce poverty and inequality, and improve the overall standard of living of the people.
marking_scheme: Definition and role of the Planning Commission (1 mark)
Explanation of the Five-Year Plans and their objectives (2 marks)
Discussion on the Commission's role in achieving the goals of planned development (2 marks)

questions.q: What are the main characteristics of India's First Five-Year Plan? Discuss its major objectives and achievements.
questions.answer: The First Five-Year Plan (1951-1956) was India's first attempt at planned development. Its main characteristics include a focus on agricultural development, irrigation, and power generation. The plan aimed to increase agricultural production, improve irrigation facilities, and generate more power. The major objectives of the plan were to achieve self-sufficiency in food production, increase industrial production, and improve the overall standard of living of the people. The plan achieved significant success in increasing agricultural production and improving irrigation facilities.
marking_scheme: Characteristics of the First Five-Year Plan (1 mark)

questions.q: Discuss the importance of community development programmes in promoting participatory development. What were the major objectives and achievements of the Community Development Programme launched in India in 1952?
questions.answer: Community development programmes are essential for promoting participatory development as they involve the active participation of local communities in the development process. The Community Development Programme launched in India in 1952 aimed to promote rural development by providing basic amenities such as healthcare, education, and sanitation. The major objectives of the programme were to improve the standard of living of rural people, promote social and economic development, and encourage community participation. The programme achieved significant success in improving rural healthcare and education facilities.
marking_scheme: Importance of community development programmes (2 marks)
Objectives of the Community Development Programme (1 mark)

questions.q: A village has a population of 5000 people. If the per capita income of the village is Rs. 8000 per annum, calculate the total annual income of the village.
questions.answer:
Calculation:
Total annual income = Per capita income x Population
= Rs. 8000 x 5000
= Rs. 40,000,000
marking_scheme: Correct calculation of total annual income (5 marks)

questions.q: If the GDP of a country is Rs. 1000 billion and its population is 100 million, calculate the per capita income of the country.
questions.answer:
Calculation:
Per capita income = GDP / Population
= Rs. 1000 billion / 100 million
= Rs. 10,000
marking_scheme: Correct calculation of per capita income (5 marks)

questions.q: A state has a literacy rate of 70%. If the total population of the state is 50 million, calculate the number of literate people in the state.
questions.answer:
Calculation:
Number of literate people = (Literacy rate / 100) x Population
= (70 / 100) x 50 million
= 35 million
marking_scheme: Correct calculation of the number of literate people (5 marks)
